tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild hypoglycemia and low HbA1c, suggesting possible recurrent low blood glucose. Elevated creatinine and significant proteinuria indicate impaired renal function, possibly nephropathy. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, but no infection markers. Findings are suggestive of renal dysfunction, possibly secondary to metabolic or systemic disease; further nephrological and metabolic evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
